key_insight: A stacking ensemble predicts welding quality metrics more accurately than a multitask neural network, while multitask learning still offers strong shared-task generalization.
engineering_relevance: The study helps select data-driven models for estimating UTS, weld hardness, and HAZ hardness to improve weld design, quality control, and process optimization.
method: Compared multitask neural network (shared hidden layers, task-specific outputs) with stacking ensemble learning.
Predicted three welding responses from a shared dataset: UTS, weld bead hardness, and HAZ hardness.
Evaluated performance using RMSE and R² across all tasks.
evidence_signal: Stacking ensemble achieved lower RMSE and higher R² for all three outputs, while MTNN showed solid but weaker performance, especially for UTS.
watch_item: Performance may depend on dataset size and feature quality, so ensemble gains may not transfer if welding conditions change or data are sparse/noisy.
paper_title: A Comparative Analysis of Multitask Neural Networks and Stacking Ensemble Learning for Predicting UTS, Weld Hardness, and HAZ Hardness in Welding Applications
